title: Connecticut woman running 2026 Boston Marathon after Alzheimer’s strikes her family again
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colleen Graham from Norwalk, Connecticut, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise awareness for Alzheimer's disease.
Graham's father was diagnosed with Alzheimer's in 2024 and her nana also had the disease, influencing her decision to run.
She aims to raise $30,000 to support Alzheimer's research and honor her family's experience with the disease.

title: New England travel tips for the last days of winter
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: South Boston St. Patrick’s Day Parade on March 15 will reverse its route; up to 1 million attendees expected.
Maine Maple Sunday on March 21-22 offers maple tours, tastings, and events across sugarhouses and farms statewide.
Halfinger Farms in Higganum, Conn., reopens April 1 with 150,000 daffodils for picking and spring plants available.

title: Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: GoFundMe launched for Crystal Johnson-Clementi's family surpassed $46,000, nearly reaching its goal.
Crystal Johnson-Clementi, 37, a Leominster bartender, died from a Southborough car accident on February 20.
Johnson-Clementi, a breast cancer survivor, is remembered for her generosity and resilience by family and friends.

title: Jayson Tatum: Active for the game, never questionable in the tunnel
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to return to TD Garden floor after a 10-month absence for the Celtics.
Teammates Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh gained attention for their fashion as Tatum recuperated.
Tatum's comeback is also generating buzz for his anticipated pre-game fashion choice.

title: How Susan Hurley’s legacy inspired her son to run the Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Max McGillivray will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late mother, Susan Hurley, and support the Justice Health Initiative.
Susan Hurley, founder of CharityTeams, raised $42 million over two decades for nonprofits and charity marathon runners.
Justice Health Initiative benefits from the marathon, providing essential healthcare to children, including conjoined twins Fati and Mariem.

title: Back Bay’s ‘ugly duckling’ is getting a makeover
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Developers Lourenco Carminati and Eagle Hill Capital are transforming 18 Commonwealth Ave. into a luxury single-family home.
The property will feature five bedrooms, a rooftop terrace with a kitchen, and a two-car garage plus additional parking.
The revamped home is expected to enter the market in May 2026, with a price range of $20 million to $24 million.

title: Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprises Boston bar crowd, picks up everyone’s tab
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Country singer Bailey Zimmerman surprised patrons at J.J. Foley's Cafe in Boston by covering the entire bar tab.
Zimmerman visited the South End pub after performing a sold-out show at TD Garden on his 'Different Night Same Rodeo Tour.'
J.J. Foley’s Cafe will kick off its 117th birthday and St. Patrick’s celebrations on Saturday with a party.

title: Haitian man living in Boston dies in ICE custody, heightening concerns about detention centers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emmanuel Damas, a 56-year-old Haitian man, died in ICE custody after inadequate medical treatment for a toothache led to sepsis.
Damas was held in Central Arizona Florence Correctional Center, operated by CoreCivic, raising concerns about medical neglect in ICE facilities.
Massachusetts lawmakers have demanded accountability from ICE and expressed concerns over the agency's treatment of detainees in custody.

title: Celtics officially list Jayson Tatum as ‘Available’ for game vs. Mavericks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum is officially listed as 'available' for the Celtics game against the Mavericks on Friday.
Tatum has been out since rupturing his Achilles tendon during the 2025 playoffs.
The Celtics are currently 41-21 and second in the Eastern Conference, exceeding preseason expectations.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to two-year deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Patriots have agreed to a two-year, $7.4 million deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito.
DeVito, a 27-year-old former New York Giants player, joined the Patriots in 2025 after being claimed off waivers.
The contract includes $2 million guaranteed, solidifying DeVito's position behind starter Drake Maye and veteran Josh Dobbs.
